oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Are You a Bird Brain?
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Staff, AFA Watchbird
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 50 Number 1 (October/November 2023).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Are You a Bird Brain? Match the term for a group of each type of bird. Geese (on ground or in water) a. Cast Peacocks b. Murmuration Hawks c. Murder Toucans d. Mob Quails e. Flamboyance Crows f. Colony Blackbirds g. Gaggle Cockatoos h. Covey or Bevy Flamingos i. Muster Penguins j. Parliament What Am I?A group of this bird is called a rookery. I am a seabird and among the largest of the flying birds. I am found over a large range in the Southern Ocean and North Pacific. The largest of my genus has the longest wingspan of any bird - around 11 feet! My wingspan allows me to ride the ocean winds and to sometimes glide for hours without resting or flapping my wings. There is disagreement as to the actual number of species, but of the 22 species recognized by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN), 21 are listedat some level of concern. We come ashore usually on remote islands to mate and a breeding season may take over a year from laying to fledging with just one egg laid. Information courtesy of Wikipedia Answers found on pge 26Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 50 No. 4 (2023): Watchbird October/November 2023; 16
